name: Bun:SQLite (Built-in SQLite for Bun Runtime)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Oven.sh / Jarred Sumner; requires Bun runtime

auth.notes: Local SQLite database; no auth; optional encryption via SQLCipher

performance.notes: Fastest SQLite binding for JavaScript; 10-15x faster than better-sqlite3 in Bun

agent_readiness.notes: Bun's built-in high-performance SQLite. import { Database } from 'bun:sqlite'. const db = new Database('mydb.sqlite'). const query = db.query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = $id'). query.get({$id: 1}). query.all({$id: 1}). db.run('INSERT INTO users (name) VALUES (?)', ['Alice']). db.prepare('INSERT INTO users (name) VALUES (?)').run('Alice'). Transaction: const tx = db.transaction((name) => { ... }); tx('Alice'). query.as(UserClass) for typed rows. db.exec() for DDL. WAL: db.exec('PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL'). Synchronous API (no async overhead). db.serialize() / db.deserialize() for in-memory ops. query.values() returns arrays instead of objects.

gotchas: Bun-only — does not work in Node.js; use better-sqlite3 for Node.js
Synchronous API — no async/await for queries (by design for performance)
Parameters must use $name prefix in SQL ($id not :id for bun:sqlite)
No migration tooling built-in — use Drizzle or custom SQL files
best_when: Local data storage in Bun CLI tools or servers
When better-sqlite3 is used in Node.js but migrating to Bun
Embedded SQLite for Bun apps without external database
summary: bun:sqlite is Bun's built-in high-performance SQLite binding. import { Database } from 'bun:sqlite'. db.query(sql).get($params) / .all($params). Transactions. 10-15x faster than better-sqlite3. Synchronous API. MIT / Bun runtime. Best for SQLite in Bun apps. Bun-only — use better-sqlite3 for Node.js. Parameters use $name prefix. No built-in migrations.
